zh.lifeSummary: 當Albrecht I. der Große Herzog von Braunschweig-Lüneburg於1236年出生在德國尼德薩辛布勞恩史維時，他的父親Otto I. gen. das Kind Herzog von Braunschweig-Lüneburg32歲，母親Markgräfin Mathilde von Brandenburg von Braunschweig Lüneburg26歲。他於1266年11月1日與Alessia di Monferrato結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1279年8月15日在德國尼德薩辛布勞恩史維布勞恩史維享年43歲，埋葬在德國尼德薩辛布勞恩史維。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Albrecht I. der Große Herzog von Braunschweig-Lüneburg于1236年出生在德国尼德薩辛布勞恩史維时，他的父亲Otto I. gen. das Kind Herzog von Braunschweig-Lüneburg32岁，母亲Markgräfin Mathilde von Brandenburg von Braunschweig Lüneburg26岁。他于1266年11月1日与Alessia di Monferrato结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1279年8月15日在德国尼德薩辛布勞恩史維布勞恩史維享年43岁，埋葬在德国尼德薩辛布勞恩史維。

nameMeanings.name: Herzog
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Erwin, Fritz, Heinz, Kurt, Otto, Wolfgang, Franz, Arno, Ernst, Hildegarde, Reinhold.

German: from the Middle High German title of nobility herzoge ‘duke’ (Old High German herizoho, from heri ‘army’ + ziohan ‘to lead’, a calque of the Byzantine title stratēlatēs ‘general, commander’, from Greek stratos ‘army’ + elaunein ‘to lead’). The name is unlikely to refer to descent from an actual duke; it is normally an occupational name for the servant of a duke or a nickname for one who put on the airs and graces of a duke. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Poland, Czechia, and Slovenia (see also 3 below). Compare Hertzog .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name from German Herzog ‘duke’ (see 1 above). Compare Graf and Kaiser .

variants.name: Hartsook
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog  .

variants.name: Herceg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Croatian Stanko.

Croatian: nickname from herceg ‘duke’, a title of the medieval feudal lords of Herzegovina (‘duke's land’, a region in Bosnia and Herzegovina), derived from the Hungarian form of German Herzog . Compare Erceg .

variants.name: Herczeg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Imre, Laszlo, Tibor, Attila, Ferenc, Gaza, Istvan, Janos.
Hungarian form of German   Herzog  .

variants.name: Herzig
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erna, Fritzi, Hans, Horst, Markus, Monika.
German (eastern and central Germany and Silesia, Switzerland): variant of   Herzog  .
German: from a short form of any of several ancient Germanic personal names composed with hard ‘strong, hardy’, for instance Herto.

variants.name: Hertzog
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of Herzog and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this. The surname Hertzog is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine).

variants.name: Hartzog
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog   1.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsock
meanings: Probably an altered form of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsoe
meanings: Perhaps an altered form of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsough
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog  .

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Albrecht
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright’.

nameMeanings.name: der
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of the given names beginning with the letter D- ( compare Jay and Kay ), in particular Dorothy . It is also used as an independent name, and may in some cases be associated with the River Dee ( compare Clyde ).

nameMeanings.name: Große
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: habitational name from Grone near Göttingen or other places so named in Lower Saxony.
North German (Gröne): variant of Grün (see Gruen ). Compare Groene .
North German: unflattering nickname for a lecher, from Middle Low German grone ‘lascivious’.

variants.name: Groner
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, in origin a local name for someone who lived near a grove of trees (Old English grāf). Use as a given name is partly due to the U.S. president (Stephen) Grover Cleveland ( 1837–1908 ).

variants.name: Grona
meanings: Variant of   Goronwy .

variants.name: Gruen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Dieter, Dietrich, Georg, Hans, Heinz, Hermann, Hertha.  Jewish Ehud, Eliezer, Isadore, Isidor, Meyer, Rivka, Shimon, Shlomo, Shoshana.

German (Grün): from Middle High German grūene ‘green, fresh, raw’, hence a nickname for someone who habitually dressed in green, a topographic name for someone who lived in a green and leafy place, or a habitational name for someone from a place called with this word, such as Gruna, Grunau in Silesia.
Jewish (Ashkenazic; Grün): artificial name from German grün ‘green’. Compare Grun .

en.lifeSummary: When Albrecht I. der Große Herzog von Braunschweig-Lüneburg was born in 1236, in Brunswick, Lower Saxony, Germany, his father, Otto I. gen. das Kind Herzog von Braunschweig-Lüneburg, was 32 and his mother, Markgräfin Mathilde von Brandenburg von Braunschweig Lüneburg, was 26. He married Alessia di Monferrato on 1 November 1266. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 15 August 1279, in Braunschweig, Brunswick, Lower Saxony, Germany, at the age of 43, and was buried in Brunswick, Lower Saxony, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Albrecht I. der Große Herzog von Braunschweig-Lüneburg est né en 1236, à Braunschweig, Niedersachsen, Allemagne, son père, Otto I. gen. das Kind Herzog von Braunschweig-Lüneburg, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Markgräfin Mathilde von Brandenburg von Braunschweig Lüneburg, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Alessia di Monferrato le 1 novembre 1266. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 15 août 1279, à Braunschweig, Braunschweig, Niedersachsen, Allemagne, à l’âge de 43 ans, et a été enterré à Braunschweig, Niedersachsen, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Albrecht I. der Große Herzog von Braunschweig-Lüneburg 1236 in Braunschweig, Niedersachsen, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Otto I. gen. das Kind Herzog von Braunschweig-Lüneburg 32 und seine Mutter Markgräfin Mathilde von Brandenburg von Braunschweig Lüneburg 26. Er hat am 1. November 1266 Alessia di Monferrato geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 15. August 1279 im Alter von 43 Jahren in Braunschweig, Braunschweig, Niedersachsen, Deutschland und wurde in Braunschweig, Niedersachsen, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Albrecht I. der Große Herzog von Braunschweig-Lüneburg è nato il nel 1236 nel Braunschweig, Niedersachsen, Germania, il suo padre, Otto I. gen. das Kind Herzog von Braunschweig-Lüneburg, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Markgräfin Mathilde von Brandenburg von Braunschweig Lüneburg, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Alessia di Monferrato il 1 novembre 1266. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 15 agosto 1279, nel Braunschweig, Braunschweig, Niedersachsen, Germania, all'età di 43 anni, e fu sepolto a Braunschweig, Niedersachsen, Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Albrecht I. der Große Herzog von Braunschweig-Lüneburgが1236年にドイツニーダーサチセンブラウンスチウエイグで生まれた時，父Otto I. gen. das Kind Herzog von Braunschweig-Lüneburgは32歳，母Markgräfin Mathilde von Brandenburg von Braunschweig Lüneburgは26歳であった。1266年11月1日にAlessia di Monferratoと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1279年8月15日にドイツニーダーサチセンブラウンスチウエイグブラウンスチウエイグで43歳で死去し，ドイツニーダーサチセンブラウンスチウエイグにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Albrecht I. der Große Herzog von Braunschweig-Lüneburg(이)가 1236년 독일 니더작센 브라슈위그에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Otto I. gen. das Kind Herzog von Braunschweig-Lüneburg(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Markgräfin Mathilde von Brandenburg von Braunschweig Lüneburg(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1266년 11월 1일 Alessia di Monferrato(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1279년 8월 15일 독일 니더작센 브라슈위그 브라슈위그에서 43세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 니더작센 브라슈위그에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Albrecht I. der Große Herzog von Braunschweig-Lüneburg nasceu em 1236, em Braunschweig, Niedersachsen, Alemanha, seu pai, Otto I. gen. das Kind Herzog von Braunschweig-Lüneburg, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Markgräfin Mathilde von Brandenburg von Braunschweig Lüneburg, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Alessia di Monferrato em 1 de novembro de 1266. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 15 de agosto de 1279, em Braunschweig, Braunschweig, Niedersachsen, Alemanha, com 43 anos, e foi sepultado em Braunschweig, Niedersachsen, Alemanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Albrecht I. der Große Herzog von Braunschweig-Lüneburg родился в 1236 г. в местности браунщвиг, ниедирсачсен, Германия, его отцу по имени Otto I. gen. das Kind Herzog von Braunschweig-Lüneburg было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Markgräfin Mathilde von Brandenburg von Braunschweig Lüneburg, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Alessia di Monferrato 1 ноября 1266 г. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер 15 августа 1279 г. в местности браунщвиг, браунщвиг, ниедирсачсен, Германия в возрасте 43 лет и был похоронен в местности браунщвиг, ниедирсачсен, Германия.

sm.Bet. 1267–1279: mai le 1267 e o'o I le 1279

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Albrecht I. der Große Herzog von Braunschweig-Lüneburg nació en 1236, en Braunschweig, Niedersachsen, Alemania, su papá, Otto I. gen. das Kind Herzog von Braunschweig-Lüneburg, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Markgräfin Mathilde von Brandenburg von Braunschweig Lüneburg, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Alessia di Monferrato el 1 de noviembre de 1266. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 15 de agosto de 1279, en Braunschweig, Braunschweig, Niedersachsen, Alemania, a la edad de 43 años, y fue sepultado en Braunschweig, Niedersachsen, Alemania.
